summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a psychedelic 1989 performance, highlighted by a soaring Dark Star and magical Attics encore.
review: The show is lauded as one of the best late‑era Dead concerts, with a second set that drips in psychedelia and a 20‑minute Dark Star that many reviewers call amazing. The SBD recording is clear enough to hear crowd reactions, though a few note missing track numbers and a cassette patch issue, but overall it delivers an excellent listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently five‑star praise for the musical performance with only minor technical complaints; strong consensus on show excellence and good soundboard quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear SBD capture with audible crowd, occasional cassette patch note and missing track numbers, but overall very listenable.

standout_songs: Playing in the Band > Uncle John's Band > Playing in the Band
show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set praised for its psychedelic flow and powerful encore, creating a magical night that many reviewers describe as unmatched.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Great vocals and tone highlighted; several reviewers note his vocal performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard work singled out on Dark Star and Attics.
